mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, it signals tight labor markets that boost consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 6.0% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and a rising federal funds rate indicate a reactive monetary tightening that surprises markets and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint increase in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.04 over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis demand recoveries, month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals expanding household consumption that lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations during the specified regime over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and earnings momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600 thousand units indicates a resurgent construction sector that drives cyclical equity outperformance, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following HOUST > 600 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with rising industrial production confirms a non-inflationary growth expansion that reduces equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling UNRATE and rising INDPRO fails to generate positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC < 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.
